texto: There’s nothing like that feeling of waiting for a guy. It’s the loneliest feeling in the world. Holding that cell phone in your hand as you take out the trash, use the bathroom, change the litter box. Fearful that the one second you aren’t looking will be when they call. Pathetic. And something I have done as recently as last week.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Hilary Winston: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de enfrentar incertezas com ação. O trecho “There’s nothing like that feeling of waiting for a guy. It’s the loneliest feeling in the world. …” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que coragem, dating, longing pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de mudanca e longing.

texto: When I was younger I was attracted to gay guys and they were "attracted" to me. I don't exactly know why...But it turned out the gay guys were the ones holding all the cards, both physically and emotionally, and I've certainly paid the price for making that mistake. And so have all of the guys I've dated since.

texto: We could love and not be suckers. We could dream and not be losers. It was such a beautiful time. Everything was possible because we didn't know anything yet.
